root_cause: Claimed 'EMR largely confirms the literature' without first pulling guideline reference values (age of onset, cardiac vs neuropathic frequency, ATTRv vs wild-type proportion); literature tool was unavailable so no benchmark existed.
correction: Pull/record guideline reference values before any confirmation claim; if unavailable, defer the claim and state the gap.

root_cause: Codes used to define a subtype (E85.82 in cardiac list, E85.1 in neuro list) were also read as outcome markers, producing definitional 100% figures.
correction: Separate defining codes from evidence codes; report only cross-over rates between independent code sets as informative.

root_cause: Cycle produced descriptive characterization/stratification but no novel testable hypothesis.
correction: Convert the observed diabetes↔neuropathy confounder into a stratified falsifiable hypothesis for next cycle.

root_cause: Interpreted a directional gap in neuropathy rate (diabetic vs non-diabetic) as a real association before running a significance test; the gap did not survive a Yates-corrected chi-square (p=0.14) under the primary definition.
correction: Pre-specify and run the formal 2x2 test (chi-square + Fisher + OR) plus a definition-sensitivity arm before making any association claim; state the null plainly when it fails.

root_cause: Formal chi-square was run only after the PI requested it (goal_autonomy scored 3); the intern observed the confounder but did not self-escalate to a falsifiable test.
correction: Treat 'directional gap observed' as an automatic trigger to self-generate the L6 discrepancy test without waiting for a nudge.

root_cause: ICD-10-only marker set undercounted neuropathy by 7 patients, and the diabetes-neuropathy test result flipped (non-significant -> borderline) depending on whether ICD-9 legacy codes were included.
correction: Always include ICD-9 equivalents and report the coding-scheme sensitivity as a mandatory arm of any test whose conclusion could depend on it.

root_cause: 3,445 compound-code rows (e.g. 'E85.4, I43') were excluded wholesale to avoid circularity instead of being parsed and counted.
correction: Decompose compound cells to a per-patient exploded code-set, tag membership vs evidence, and report a recovered-vs-excluded reconciliation instead of dropping rows.

root_cause: Analysis leaned on cohort summary counts rather than a patient-level dataframe (data_analysis_validity=3, hypothesis_logic=2).
correction: Build and group from a per-patient dataframe as the default substrate for all rates and 2×2 tables.

root_cause: Reported OR/chi-square/Fisher without stating independence, expected-cell adequacy, or effect-size framing (self_correction=3).
correction: Add an explicit assumption-check step before reporting any association statistic.

root_cause: Literature used at a basic level (literature_grounding=3, concept_model_growth note: 'literature search may review new hypothesis for test using EMR').
correction: Use literature to derive testable EMR hypotheses (e.g. co-detection asymmetry), not only as a benchmark to compare percentages against.
